title: Crisp Steamed Korean Sirloin Steak Songpyeon
description: Rich, silky Korean dish featuring sirloin steak, brine-dried, whole30-friendly, steamed to perfection in a songpyeon form.
instructions: Prepare a brine solution by dissolving 1 teaspoon of salt in 1 cup of water, and add 1/2 teaspoon of black pepper. Let it cool to room temperature.
In a small bowl, mix together 2 cloves of minced garlic, 1 tablespoon of grated ginger, and 1/4 teaspoon of Korean chili flakes (if using).
Add the garlic-ginger mixture to the cooled brine solution and stir to combine.
Place the sirloin steak in the brine solution and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Preheat a cataplana or a large skillet over medium-high heat. Remove the sirloin steak from the brine solution and pat it dry with paper towels.

Season the sirloin steak with salt and black pepper. Add 2 tablespoons of sesame oil to the preheated cataplana or skillet and sear the sirloin steak for 2-3 minutes per side, or until it reaches your desired level of doneness.
Transfer the sirloin steak to a plate and let it rest for 5 minutes.
Meanwhile, prepare the songpyeon form by brushing it with sesame oil and seasoning it with salt and black pepper.
Place the songpyeon form in a steamer basket and steam it over high heat for 2-3 minutes, or until it's lightly toasted.
Place the rested sirloin steak in the songpyeon form and serve immediately.
For a more authentic experience, serve the sirloin steak with a side of kimchi or other Korean fermented vegetables.
